stories.title: Iranians are leaving the country just to access the internet
stories.description: Iran has cut off its access to the global internet. To find an internet connection, some Iranians are traveling across the border with Turkey — even just to make video calls and then go back home.

stories.title: Greetings from an Islamabad park, a peaceful vantage point in an uncertain world
stories.description: The park, near the venue where inconclusive Iran-U.S. peace talks took place this month, provides respite to those who visit it.

stories.title: Ships attacked in Strait of Hormuz. And, VA voters approve redistricting effort
stories.description: Two ships came under attack in the Strait of Hormuz, hours after Trump extended the ceasefire with Iran. And, Virginia voters approved a measure allowing Democrats to redraw the congressional map.

stories.title: Iran says it seized ships in Strait of Hormuz as U.S. blockade continues amid ceasefire
stories.description: Three vessels came under fire in the Strait of Hormuz, after the U.S. and Iran failed to meet in Pakistan for talks to end the war and as President Trump extended the ceasefire but kept the blockade.

stories.title: As Pakistan seeks to broker U.S.–Iran peace, citizens watch for gains at home
stories.description: As Pakistan works to broker peace between the U.S. and Iran, many Pakistanis are watching closely to see whether the diplomatic boost will translate into improvements at home.

stories.title: What would happen next if Iran agreed to surrender its highly enriched uranium
stories.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Scott Roecker of the Nuclear Threat Initiative about how the U.S. might go about moving highly enriched uranium out of Iran.

stories.title: Trump extends U.S. ceasefire with Iran at Pakistan's request
stories.description: The extension was announced just hours before it was set to expire. The president did not provide details on how long the new ceasefire extension will last.

stories.title: Lebanon accuses Israel of targeting journalist killed in air strike
stories.description: Lebanon's prime minister accused Israel of war crimes after IDF attacks on Red Cross vehicles also stopped rescuers from reaching the site.

stories.title: Iran says it has seized two ships in Strait of Hormuz after vessels attacked
stories.description: One of the cargo ships was 'heavily damaged' by Iranian forces, a British maritime agency reported on Wednesday.

stories.title: Trump buys time for Iran deal after frantic day of diplomacy
stories.description: The US president's decision marked the second time in as many weeks he has backed off a threat to escalate the war, buying more time.

stories.title: Extension of US-Iran ceasefire gives 'breathing space'
stories.description: The BBC's chief international correspondent Lyse Doucet reports from Tehran as Trump announces an extension of the ceasefire with Iran.

stories.title: Iran sees mass redundancies from war with US and Israel
stories.description: The impact is being felt by manufacturers, retailers and the digital sector, amid warnings it could get worse if the war resumes.

stories.title: Israeli soldiers punished over vandalism of Jesus statue in Lebanon
stories.description: Two soldiers will be removed from combat duty and receive 30 days of military detention, Israel's military says.

stories.title: US intercepts and seizes Iranian-flagged cargo ship, Trump says
stories.description: Tehran vows retaliation soon for what it calls an "act of armed piracy", which comes as the US prepares for a second round of talks.

stories.title: Trump says US will not lift Hormuz blockade until deal made with Iran
stories.description: The US president's comments come amid uncertainty over whether Iran will attend peace talks in Pakistan this week.

stories.title: Tehran will never cede control of Strait of Hormuz, senior Iranian politician tells BBC
stories.description: Lyse Doucet speaks to Ebrahim Azizi, who says Iran "will decide the right of passage" through the crucial shipping route.

stories.title: Why and how is US blockading Iranian ports in Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: Donald Trump says that the US is blockading the Strait of Hormuz. What does this mean in practice?

stories.title: Pentagon removes top Navy official amid military shake-up
stories.description: US Secretary of the Navy John Phelan has left his post with immediate effect, the Pentagon said Wednesday, without providing an explanation. His departure comes amid a broader clear-out of senior military figures under President Donald Trump, as the United States remains locked i...

stories.title: Middle East war live: Lebanon meets Israel in Washington to request truce extension
stories.description: Israel and Lebanon hold a new round of talks in Washington on Thursday, during which Beirut plans to request a one-month extension of a ceasefire due to expire within days. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

stories.title: Sheinbaum weighs sanctions on Chihuahua state after CIA agents died after drug lab raid in Mexico
stories.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said Wednesday that she's considering possible sanctions against the government of Chihuahua — a state bordering Texas — for allowing CIA agents to participate in an operation to dismantle drug laboratories, because any security collaboration w...

stories.title: EU unveils plan to tackle energy crisis
stories.description: The European Union has unveiled plans to shield households and businesses from the energy crisis, triggered by the war with Iran. The bloc says it has spent an additional €24 billion on energy imports beacuse of higher prices since the war began, and it urged more investment in r...

stories.title: ‘Hungry all the time’: US Navy deny food shortages after grim photos emerge
stories.description: The US Navy has fiercely denied claims that its warships in the Middle East are running low on food and leaving its sailors “hungry all the time,” as Washington’s blockade in the Strait of Hormuz continues. Photos showing dismal, mostly-empty trays of food circulated online, with...

stories.title: 'Right now both sides are testing each other', analyst says as Iran truce extended
stories.description: FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with Negah Angha, Former National Security Advisor under US President Joe Biden, about the ongoing standoff between Iran and the United States, which has choked off all traffic through the Strait of Hormuz. She says that "right now both sides are test...

stories.title: French soldier dies of wounds after attack on UN force in Lebanon
stories.description: President Emmanuel Macron said on Wednesday that a second French soldier had died following an attack on United Nations peacekeepers in Lebanon last week, ​which ‌he said was carried out by Iran-backed Hezbollah. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with the spokesperson of the UN peacek...

stories.title: Three ships targeted in Hormuz, Iran seizes two
stories.description: Iranian forces targeted three container ships on Wednesday, seizing two, the latest incidents to threaten a crucial trade route in the Middle East war. Iran's parliament speaker said on Wednesday that the Islamic republic would not reopen the Strait of Hormuz as long as the US na...

stories.title: Protesters stabbed at London rally against war on Iran
stories.description: Videos show multiple individuals with what appear to be knife wounds, who were taking part in a protest in London.

stories.title: Israel kills journalist and wounds another in south Lebanon targeted attack
stories.description: Israel has killed journalist Amal Khalil and injured Zeinab Faraj in a ‘double-tap’ attack in southern Lebanon.

stories.title: Iran releases video of IRGC seizing ship in the Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Iran’s revolutionary guard has released a video said to show its forces seizing a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Iran war live: Israel kills Lebanese journalist; Tehran-US talks stalled
stories.description: Israeli attacks on Lebanon killed at least five people on Wednesday, including Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil.

stories.title: Iran blames Trump’s blockade for diplomatic impasse as fragile truce holds
stories.description: Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf says US and Israel will not achieve their goals 'through bullying'.

stories.title: Israeli airstrike kills journalist in south Lebanon: civil defence
stories.description: An Israeli airstrike killed a Lebanese journalist and wounded another on Wednesday while they were working near the border with Israel, according to their employer and rescuers. The civil defence agency’s press office said rescuers were “able to recover the body of martyred journ...

stories.title: Lebanon, Israel to meet today as truce hangs by a thread
stories.description: • Envoys’ meeting in Washington aims to extend ceasefire, halt Israel’s demolitions• Beirut sees extension as first step to talks on forces’ withdrawal, return of detainees• Israeli strikes kill five in Lebanon, including a journalist WASHINGTON: Lebanese and Israeli envoys are s...

stories.title: US optimistic about Iran talks ‘this week’
stories.description: • Trump says resumption of negotiations in Islamabad possible within ‘36 to 72 hours’; appreciates Tehran for stopping ‘execution’ of detained women• Pezeshkian welcomes dialogue, cites ‘breach of commitments, blockade and threats’ as main obstacles• Trump denies media reports ab...

stories.title: Iran appreciates Pakistan’s efforts to mediate between Tehran and Washington
stories.description: Iran’s foreign ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei said on Wednesday that the Islamic republic appreciated Pakistan’s efforts to end the Middle East war, without commenting on a ceasefire extension announced by the United States. He expressed these views while answering reporters’ ...

stories.title: Indonesia denounces Israel ‘propaganda’ banner over Gaza hospital ruins
stories.description: Indonesia on Wednesday accused Israel of flying a “propaganda” banner over the ruins of a hospital that had been built in Gaza with Indonesian funding. The Indonesia Hospital, in the north of the Gaza Strip near the fortified border with Israel, was opened in late 2015 after Indo...

stories.title: Ink and rubble: The systematic assault on Iran’s intellectual soul
stories.description: Last year, my plans to celebrate Eid in Iran were dismantled by the sudden escalation of regional aggression. What was meant to be a personal pilgrimage became a casualty of geopolitics. Yet, in a twist of fate this past September, I found myself touching down at Tehran’s IKA Air...

stories.title: Israel-Iran LIVE: Iran attacks 3 ships in Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Lebanon meets Israel in Washington to request truce extension

stories.title: Watch: Donald Trump should lift the blockade | The Hindu Editorial
stories.description: If Donald Trump wants a negotiated exit, he should lift the blockade in return for Iran easing its restrictions on the Strait of Hormuz. Tehran, too, should step back from maximalist positions and be ready to make concessions on the nuclear front in exchange for sanctions relief ...

stories.title: Iran seizes two ships in Strait of Hormuz after Trump extends ceasefire
stories.description: One Israel-linked vessel among the two that ‘endangered maritime security’, says IRGC; Trump says ceasefire extended until Iran comes up with peace proposal, Tehran yet to confirm extension

stories.title: Iran-Israel war updates: Iran says won't reopen Hormuz as long as U.S. blockade remains
stories.description: Iran Guards say 'seized' two ships attempting to cross Strait of Hormuz; Iran says it has yet to decide whether to join a new round of talks with the U.S.

stories.title: Israeli strikes kill 3 in Lebanon, Beirut to seek truce extension
stories.description: Ahead of the talks on Thursday (April 23, 2026), Israel called on the Lebanese government to "work together" with it against Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah

stories.title: Watch: Tamil Nadu and West Bengal to decide their fate tomorrow | Above the Fold | 22.04.2016
stories.description: In today’s video, we cover the upcoming Tamil Nadu and West Bengal Assembly elections as attention turns to polling day. We also bring you the latest on the Iran–Israel conflict, a significant court ruling in the 2006 Malegaon blast case, and revisit Pahalgam a year after the att...

stories.title: US forces 31 vessels to turn around since start of naval blockade of Iran — command
stories.description: According to the Iranian state broadcaster, Tehran does not intend to comply with Washington’s unilaterally announced extension of the ceasefire and will prioritize its own interests

stories.title: US redirects 29 ships since start of naval blockade of Iran — CENTCOM
stories.description: The Central Command of the US Armed Forces denied that several commercial ships had evaded the blockade

stories.title: Poor shipping in Hormuz, Iran blockade affect Russia — deputy foreign minister
stories.description: Alexander Pankin stressed that the current situation is extremely difficult

stories.title: Iran accuses Bahrain, Qatar, Kuwait, UAE, Saudi Arabia of complicity with US
stories.description: Iran's Permanent Representative to the United Nations Amir-Saeid Iravani stressed that the US uses the territory and airspace of these states to plan, prepare and carry out illegal military attacks against the Islamic Republic
